notes: Mock Mode preserves the exact same invitation, approval, and source-label flow as live mode.
Mock payload is demo-safe and intentionally excludes raw personal context.

privacy_broker.name: Local Privacy Broker
privacy_broker.summary: Local-first suggestion. User approval required. Capability card only.

capability_invitation.title: Capability Invitation
capability_invitation.headline: AirJelly suggests a focus check-in helper for your HumanMCP toolbox.
capability_invitation.reason_summary: A private local signal suggests you are in a deep-work block and may want a low-noise check-in endpoint.
capability_invitation.approved_copy: User approved. Endpoint added to HumanMCP toolbox.

endpoint_card.summary: Checks whether the operator wants a focus-preserving check-in before routing work.

operator_narration: AirJelly does not dump private memories into HumanMCP.
It suggests a capability locally, asks for approval, and only the approved capability card reaches the toolbox.
This works in both live mode and Mock Mode.
forbidden_claims: AirJelly silently turns users into tools without approval.
The demo depends on live AirJelly connectivity to prove the story.
